name: domain-model
purpose: Prevent GenAI from confusing critical domain concepts when generating decision screens.

Action.definition: Owned next step required to resolve risk, progress recommendation, close proof gap or prepare customer interaction.
Action.runtimeRule: Every generated action must show owner, due date and priority.

Recommendation.definition: Evidence-aware proposal for what should be considered or done next.
Recommendation.runtimeRule: A recommendation may create actions, but is not an owned action by itself.

Evidence.definition: Information that supports a signal, risk, recommendation, proof point or decision.
Evidence.runtimeRule: Evidence is not proof by itself and must not be replaced by AI confidence.

Proof.definition: Evidence that can support a value claim, customer review, renewal conversation or business impact narrative.
Proof.runtimeRule: Proof must be grounded in evidence and labeled by readiness.

ExpectedOutcome.definition: Projected, estimated or illustrative value that has not yet been proven.
ExpectedOutcome.runtimeRule: Expected outcome must not be presented as proven value.

SourceStrength.definition: Strength of the source context behind evidence, not confidence in an AI answer.
SourceStrength.runtimeRule: Never use AI confidence as source strength.

AssetHealth.definition: Observed or inferred state of an asset at a point in time.
AssetHealth.runtimeRule: Asset health is a signal, not the full asset intelligence narrative.

AssetIntelligence.definition: Evidence-aware interpretation of asset signals, context, risks and recommended follow-through.
AssetIntelligence.runtimeRule: Asset intelligence must show scope, evidence and recommended follow-through.

CustomerReadiness.definition: Whether information is suitable to expose to a customer.
CustomerReadiness.runtimeRule: Customer readiness is not validation status.

ValidationStatus.definition: Review state of information before it is trusted or customer-facing.
ValidationStatus.runtimeRule: Validation status must remain visible when customer use is sensitive.

InternalProof.definition: Evidence-backed proof that is not ready for customer use.
InternalProof.runtimeRule: Internal proof must not be shown as customer-ready proof.

CustomerReadyProof.definition: Proof with validation and customer readiness explicit.
CustomerReadyProof.runtimeRule: Customer-ready proof needs validation status and source context.

criticalDistinctions.rule: Recommendation can propose what to do; Action is the owned follow-through.
Evidence supports proof, but evidence alone is not a value proof.
blockedConfusions: evidence called proof without proof readiness

criticalDistinctions.rule: Source strength describes source support, not model confidence.
Expected outcomes are projections and require proofReadiness expected-only.
blockedConfusions: expected outcome presented as proven value
projected saving presented as achieved saving

criticalDistinctions.rule: Asset health is a signal; asset intelligence combines signal, context, evidence, risk and next action.
blockedConfusions: health status presented as full intelligence
asset intelligence without evidence or follow-through

criticalDistinctions.rule: Customer readiness is audience suitability; validation status is review state.
Internal proof may exist but must not be exposed as customer-ready proof.

forbiddenPhrases: expected outcome presented as proven value
